agent_raw:
The migration packet does not fairly establish that the graph detector is ready to replace the existing system. Four benchmark defects prevent a clean model comparison.

First, the transaction feed changed. The baseline numbers come from the nightly batch settlement feed used in ops dashboards. The challenger graph model runs on a deduplicated authorization stream after merchant entity resolution. These are different populations of transactions — the denominator changed with the model.

Second, the label maturation rule changed. The baseline figures include transactions with both settled and still-pending dispute outcomes, consistent with how the ops dashboard tracks fraud over time. The challenger is evaluated only on transactions with settled dispute outcomes after a 60-day maturation window. This restriction systematically excludes recent fraudulent transactions that have not yet moved through the dispute pipeline, changing the positive-label population.

Third, the Q4 threshold was calibrated on Q4 volume and transaction characteristics, then reported on that same Q4 data. Selecting a threshold to maximize precision at a fixed alert volume on the evaluation quarter and reporting precision at that threshold on the same quarter inflates the apparent precision gain.

Fourth, the migration bundles the graph model with a new authorization-stream pipeline and real-time scoring path. Any precision gain could be attributable to the model, the feed, the deduplication, or the entity resolution — these are not separable from the reported metrics.

To establish a clean comparison, both systems must be rescored on the same deduplicated authorization feed with one shared 60-day maturity rule, using thresholds fixed from earlier quarters.
all_issues_raised: Transaction feed changed from settlement batch to deduplicated authorization stream
Label maturation changed from full dispute stream to 60-day settled-only
Q4 threshold calibrated on Q4 and reported on Q4 — circular
Model class and pipeline changed together — gains not attributable to model alone
